engineGuide.h2: Ford Transit Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Ford Transit across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2L Diesel
entries.history: The dominant Transit diesel of the Mk6 era, fitted to over 60% of UK-registered Transits. Fleet workhorse known for durability but prone to specific component failures.
compatibleVariants: Transit 260, Transit 280, Transit 300, Transit 350, Mk6 Panel Van, Mk6 Chassis Cab, Transit Crew Cab
commonFailures: Injector module failure - common cause of no-start and rough running
Oil pump priming issues - can lead to complete engine failure if not addressed
EGR valve clogging - affects performance and emissions
entries.cta: Get quotes for P5AT Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 2L Diesel
entries.history: A variant of the Duratorq Puma engine family used across Transit and Ford Ranger platforms. Known for sharing components with Land Rover Defender applications.
compatibleVariants: Transit V363 (FAD, FBD, FCD, FDD), Transit Bus (FD_, FB_, FS_, FZ_, FC_), Transit Van (FA_), Transit Platform/Chassis (FM_, FN_, FF_)
commonFailures: Water pump failure - known to fail around 60,000-80,000 miles
Injector seal leakage - diesel leak into engine oil
entries.cta: Get quotes for DRRA Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: The first-generation EcoBlue that replaced Duratorq from 2016. Brought reduced emissions, AdBlue injection, and improved fuel economy to the Transit range.
compatibleVariants: Transit V363 (FAD, FBD, FCD, FDD), Transit 350, Transit 400, Transit 440, Transit Custom, Transit Chassis Cab
commonFailures: Diesel injector clogging - Ford issued service action for 2019-built vehicles
DPF regeneration issues - common with short journeys
EGR cooler failure - leads to coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for YFAT Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: A higher-output tune of the EcoBlue engine producing 128hp. Fitted extensively to Transit V363 vans and chassis cab variants from 2016 onward.
compatibleVariants: Transit V363 (FAD, FBD, FCD, FDD), Transit V363 Chassis Cab (FED, FFD), Transit 400, Transit 440
commonFailures: Blocked injectors - rough running, high fuel consumption
AdBlue system crystallization - leads to warning lights and reduced performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for BKFA Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: The highest power factory tune of the first-generation EcoBlue at 167hp. Used in heavy-duty Transit applications requiring maximum towing capacity.
compatibleVariants: Transit V363 (FAD, FBD, FCD, FDD), Transit V363 Chassis Cab (FED, FFD), Transit 440, Transit 460
commonFailures: Turbocharger failure - high-mileage units (80k+ miles) at risk
Timing chain stretch - rattle on cold start
entries.cta: Get quotes for BLFA Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel MHEV
entries.history: A mild-hybrid EcoBlue variant introduced in 2019. Uses a belt-integrated starter generator for energy recovery and stop-start functionality, improving urban fuel economy.
compatibleVariants: Transit V363 (FAD, FBD, FCD, FDD), Transit V363 Chassis Cab (FED, FFD), Transit Custom mHEV

entries.title: hybrid components costly to replace
entries.cta: Get quotes for BKFC Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: A later-generation high-power EcoBlue producing 182hp. Fitted to post-2019 Transit V363 models with updated emissions calibration for Euro 6.2 compliance.
compatibleVariants: Transit V363 (FAD, FBD, FCD, FDD), Transit V363 Chassis Cab (FED, FFD), Transit Custom, Transit 460
commonFailures: Injector failure - symptoms include rough idle and poor acceleration
SCR catalyst issues - MOT emissions compliance concerns
entries.cta: Get quotes for BCFB Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 4L Diesel
entries.history: The robust 2.4-litre diesel fitted to Mk5 and early Mk6 Transits. Known for mechanical simplicity compared to later common-rail units, but now a legacy engine for older van owners.
compatibleVariants: Transit 280, Transit 300, Transit 350, Mk5 Panel Van, Mk5 Chassis Cab
commonFailures: Oil pump failure - catastrophic engine damage
Injector pump seal leakage - diesel in engine oil
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattle on startup
entries.cta: Get quotes for D6HA Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 2L Diesel
entries.history: The largest Duratorq fitted to the Transit, offering 200hp for heavy-duty applications. Used primarily in 350 and 460 payload variants for maximum towing capacity.
compatibleVariants: Transit 350 (Heavy Duty), Transit 460, Transit V363 Chassis Cab
commonFailures: Oil pump drive failure - sudden loss of oil pressure
Injector seal failure - high-pressure diesel leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for XLT Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 2L Diesel
entries.history: A Euro 6-calibrated variant of the 3.2 Duratorq fitted to 2015-onwards models with updated emissions equipment and DPF.
commonFailures: DPF clogging - especially in urban use
Turbo actuator failure - loss of boost pressure
entries.cta: Get quotes for WFAT Ford Transit engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: The rare petrol option in the Transit range, using the EcoBoost turbocharged platform. Low search volume but consistent demand from urban fleets and clean air zone operators.
compatibleVariants: Transit V363 Panel Van, Transit Custom (limited production)
commonFailures: Coolant loss from engine block - TSB issued for early examples
Carbon buildup on intake valves - direct injection engine
entries.cta: Get quotes for NEFA Ford Transit engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Ford Transit Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Ford Transit is Britain's most popular light commercial vehicle, but the 2.2L Duratorq (P5AT) and 2.0L EcoBlue (YFAT) diesel engines have well-documented weak points that drive replacement demand. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EnginesMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common Transit engine failures, what repair costs look like at a dealer versus a specialist, and when replacement is the only sensible option.

problems.group: Ford Transit 2.2 Duratorq Oil Pump Failure
problems.h4: Ford Transit 2.2 Duratorq Oil Pump Failure - Why Mk6 V348 Engines Fail Suddenly
problems.affectedModels: Ford Transit Mk6 V348 2.2 TDCi (2006-2014, P5AT, P5AT-LE, P5AT-LF)
problems.rootCause: The oil pump drive hex key wears down over time, eventually stripping completely. Oil pressure drops to zero without warning - no low oil pressure light activation until catastrophic failure has already occurred.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump, hex drive key, oil pickup seal. Sump removal required.
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early. No engine damage yet. Adds 50k-80k miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Duratorq with upgraded oil pump drive. Fully bench tested.
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty. Upgraded components prevent repeat failure.

problems.recommendation: There is no warning with this failure - one cold start, then a seized engine. If your Transit is above 80k miles on its original pump, preventative replacement is excellent value. But most owners discover the failure when it's already too late. On a Transit worth £3,000-£6,000, a rebuilt engine with upgraded pump components is the most cost-effective long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt P5AT engine prices for your Ford Transit

problems.h4: Ford Transit 2.2 & 2.0 Diesel Injector Failure - Rough Running & No-Start
problems.affectedModels: Ford Transit Mk6 (P5AT, 2006-2014), Mk7/Mk8 (YFAT, CY14, BKFA, 2014-2024)
problems.rootCause: Injector seals fail allowing combustion gases into the fuel return system, or piezoelectric injector modules fail internally. Diesel leaks into engine oil causes dilution, bearing damage, and eventual failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed injector only. New seal kit and leak-off pipes.
repairOptions.longevity: Buy time for 10k-20k miles. Other injectors likely to follow.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors. New seals, fuel filter, oil change if diluted.
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable for 60k-80k miles. Best value if catching early.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with tested injectors. Complete oil system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Required if injector failure caused bearing damage or hydraulic lock.

problems.recommendation: If caught early - before engine oil smells of diesel - a full injector set replacement is the right call. But many Transit owners discover the problem when a seized injector has already damaged the piston or bore. Get a compression test before committing to injector-only repair. If compression is compromised, go straight to a rebuilt engine.
problems.cta: Compare fuel system quotes for your Ford Transit engine code

problems.group: Ford Transit 2.0 EcoBlue EGR & DPF Clogging
problems.h4: Ford Transit 2.0 EcoBlue EGR & DPF Clogging - MOT Failure & Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Ford Transit Mk8 2.0 EcoBlue (2016-2024, YFAT, YFAR, BKFA, BLFA, BCFB)
problems.rootCause: Short journeys prevent passive DPF regeneration. EGR cooler passages clog with soot. Combined failure triggers permanent limp mode and MOT emissions failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic forced regen using dealer-level scan tool.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix only. Failure returns if driving habits unchanged.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and professionally clean EGR cooler and DPF. Relearn adaptations.
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 30k-50k miles if driving pattern improves.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EcoBlue with cleaned or replaced EGR/DPF system.
repairOptions.longevity: Required when soot ingress has damaged turbo or piston rings.

problems.recommendation: On a high-mileage Transit that does mostly urban work, EGR and DPF problems are inevitable. A professional clean buys time, but if the vehicle has already gone into limp mode multiple times, the turbo and bottom end may have suffered. For fleet operators with TCO targets, a rebuilt engine with the full emissions system overhauled is often cheaper than repeated cleanings across 12 months.
problems.cta: Compare Ford Transit EcoBlue engine replacement costs

problems.group: Ford Transit 2.4 & 3.2 Duratorq Injector Pump Failure
problems.h4: Ford Transit 2.4 & 3.2 Duratorq Injector Pump Failure - Sudden No-Start
problems.rootCause: The high-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) internal cam and plunger wear, sending metallic debris through the entire fuel system. Contamination destroys all injectors and fuel rails.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - HPFP & Full Fuel System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, all injectors, fuel rail, lines, tank flush, filter.
repairOptions.longevity: Entire fuel system must be replaced. No partial repair possible.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Duratorq with new HPFP and injectors. Bench tested.
repairOptions.longevity: Required if debris reached crankcase oil or caused bearing damage.

problems.recommendation: Once the HPFP fails, metallic contamination spreads everywhere. There is no minor repair for this failure. On older Mk5 Transits (2.4L), the vehicle's value often makes a rebuilt engine borderline - compare quotes carefully. On 3.2L Mk7 Transits used for heavy towing, the van is worth saving. A rebuilt engine with a new HPFP is the definitive fix.
problems.cta: Compare XLT and D6HA engine replacement prices

problems.h4: Ford Transit 2.0 EcoBlue Turbocharger Failure - Loss of Power & Blue Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Ford Transit Mk8 2.0 EcoBlue high-output (2016-2024, BLFA, BCFB, 167-182hp versions)
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo vanes seize due to soot buildup. Oil seal failure follows, dumping engine oil into the intake and exhaust. Unchecked failure leads to engine runaway.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo unit, oil feed and drain pipes. New oil and filter.
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable for 60k-80k miles if engine oil was changed regularly.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EcoBlue with new turbo, oil system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Required if turbo debris entered intercooler or oil starvation damaged bottom end.

problems.recommendation: A failed turbo on a high-output EcoBlue often sends metal fragments through the intake system. A simple turbo swap may leave debris in the intercooler that destroys the new unit within weeks. If the turbo failed catastrophically (broken turbine wheel), go straight to a rebuilt engine. If it's early-stage vane sticking only, a turbo replacement is fine. Ask your specialist to scope the intercooler before quoting.
problems.cta: Compare Ford Transit EcoBlue turbo replacement costs

problems.group: Ford Transit 2.4 Duratorq Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Ford Transit 2.4 Duratorq Head Gasket Failure - Coolant Loss & Overheating
problems.affectedModels: Ford Transit Mk5 (D6HA, 2000-2006), higher mileage examples over 120k miles
problems.rootCause: The original composite head gasket deteriorates with age and heat cycles. Coolant leaks externally or into cylinders. Overheating causes cylinder head warping on the iron-block, alloy-head design.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace head gasket, pressure test head, skim if warped. New bolts.
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 50k-80k miles if head is not cracked.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt D6HA with upgraded MLS head gasket. Fully tested.
repairOptions.longevity: Required if cylinder wall damage or head cracking found.

problems.recommendation: On an older Mk5 Transit, the vehicle's value rarely exceeds £2,000-£3,000. A head gasket replacement at a specialist is often viable. But if the engine has overheated multiple times, the cylinder head is likely cracked between valve seats - common on D6HA. Pay for a crack test before authorising head gasket work. If cracks are found, a rebuilt short block is the only reliable path.
problems.cta: Compare Ford Transit 2.4 Duratorq engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ford Transit
finalCta.paragraph: The Ford Transit is your business lifeline - downtime costs money, and main dealer prices for engine work can feel like a write-off. But a rebuilt engine from a specialist supplier typically costs 40-60% less than dealer pricing, includes upgraded components that address the original design flaws, and carries a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ford Transit Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford Transit and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Ford Transit Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.backDescription: Fully stripped and rebuilt to manufacturer specifications using new OE-quality components. Ideal for Transit owners planning to keep their van for years.
backBullets: Upgraded components address known P5AT and YFAT weak points
24-month unlimited mileage warranty available through select specialists

types.backDescription: The damaged engine is stripped and only failed components are replaced. A practical choice for Mk6 and Mk7 Transits with localised failure.
backBullets: Typically includes new gaskets, seals and timing components
Best value when bottom end remains within specification

types.backDescription: Sourced from rear-end or non-engine write-offs. A budget-friendly option for older Transits where full rebuild cost exceeds vehicle value.
backBullets: Compression tested and video-documented on request
No warranty on pre-existing latent failures - buyer beware

types.backDescription: Stripped to bare block, chemically cleaned, then remanufactured using new pistons, liners, bearings and oil pump. Designed for fleets needing maximum uptime.
backBullets: Includes new water pump, oil pump and timing kit
Transferable warranty - adds value at fleet resale

types.backDescription: Engine is removed, cleaned externally, compression tested and only the confirmed failed parts are replaced. A short-term fix for cosmetically tired vans.
backBullets: 30-90 day warranty typical - read terms carefully
Does not include bottom-end rebuild or crank regrind
Only viable when failure is top-end only (injectors, turbo, head gasket)

types.backDescription: Turnkey solution for van owners and fleet managers without workshop access. Fitters experienced with Transit engine swaps across Mk6, Mk7 and Mk8 chassis. All engine types include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty when sourced through EngineMarket. Prices are typical UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network and depend on exact engine code, variant, mileage and supplier. Use our free comparison tool above - enter your registration to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford Transit within hours.
backBullets: Collection and delivery options for work vans
Mobile fitting available for panel vans within M25 and major cities
All work guaranteed - labour included in warranty terms

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty when sourced through EngineMarket. Prices are typical UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network and depend on exact engine code, variant, mileage and supplier. engineSizes.h2: Ford Transit Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Ford Transit across all UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years, power outputs and common failure notes.

items.title: Ford Transit 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L EcoBlue family arrived in 2016, replacing the 2.2L Duratorq in Mk8 and V363 Transits. Available in multiple power outputs from 105hp to 182hp, it introduced AdBlue injection and reduced CO2 emissions for urban fleet compliance.
commonFailurePoints: turbocharger seal failure on high-output variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Transit 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Transit 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the Mk6 Transit range and the single most searched Transit engine size in the UK. The Duratorq Puma family powered over 60% of UK-registered Transits between 2006 and 2014, earning a reputation for hard-working reliability despite specific component weak points.
commonFailurePoints: oil pump drive hex key wear
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Transit 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Transit 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legacy diesel fitted to Mk5 and early Mk6 Transits from 2000 to 2006. Mechanically simpler than later common-rail units, the 2.4L Duratorq remains popular among older van owners and those seeking a rebuild-friendly engine without complex electronics.
commonFailurePoints: head gasket failure on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Transit 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Transit 3.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The largest diesel ever fitted to the Ford Transit, reserved for heavy-duty 350 and 460 payload variants. The 3.2L Duratorq XLT produced 200hp and was designed for max towing capacity, though production ended in 2019 when the 2.0L EcoBlue took over top-duty duties.
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Transit 3.2L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Transit 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The rare petrol option in the Transit range, using Ford's turbocharged EcoBoost platform. Low search volume but consistent demand from urban fleets, clean air zone operators, and owners of the short-lived Transit Custom petrol variant.
commonFailurePoints: Coolant loss from engine block (TSB issued)
items.cta: Compare Ford Transit 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Transit 2.0L MHEV Engines
items.description: Mild hybrid EcoBlue introduced in 2019, using a belt-integrated starter generator for energy recovery and enhanced stop-start. Designed for urban fleets needing reduced fuel consumption without full electrification. Not sure which engine size your Ford Transit needs? fuelTypes.h2: Ford Transit Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Ford Transit engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 2.4L Duratorq diesels to the latest 2.0L EcoBlue MHEV.

items.title: Ford Transit Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel has dominated Transit powertrains across Mk5, Mk6, Mk7 and Mk8 generations - with the 2.2L Duratorq and 2.0L EcoBlue families driving the highest UK replacement enquiry volumes of any commercial vehicle.
items.descriptor: Diesel has dominated Transit powertrains across Mk5, Mk6, Mk7 and Mk8 generations - with the 2.2L Duratorq and 2.0L EcoBlue families driving the highest UK replacement enquiry volumes of any commercial vehicle.
families: P5AT - 2.2L Duratorq, Mk6 workhorse with injector and oil pump failures
YFAT - 2.0L EcoBlue first generation, AdBlue-equipped from 2016
BKFA/BLFA - 2.0L EcoBlue higher outputs (128-167hp) for 350/460 payloads
D6HA - 2.4L Duratorq, Mk5 legacy diesel, mechanically simple
XLT/WFAT - 3.2L Duratorq, heavy-duty 200hp option for max towing
foundIn: Transit Mk6 V348 (2006-2014) - 2.2L P5AT series, 2.4L D6HA
Transit Mk8 V363 (2016-2024) - 2.0L YFAT, BKFA, BLFA, BCFB
knownFor: High-volume UK commercial vehicle - 60%+ of Mk6 Transits run 2.2L Duratorq
Oil pump drive failure on P5AT - sudden failure without warning
Injector seal and module failures across all diesel generations
Excellent towing capability on 3.2L heavy-duty variants
DPF and EGR clogging on short-journey EcoBlue vans
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Transit Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Ford Transit Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Transits are rare in the UK, with only the 2.0L EcoBoost offered in limited numbers from 2019 - primarily sought by urban fleets and clean air zone operators avoiding diesel restrictions.
items.descriptor: Petrol Transits are rare in the UK, with only the 2.0L EcoBoost offered in limited numbers from 2019 - primarily sought by urban fleets and clean air zone operators avoiding diesel restrictions.
knownFor: Very low UK search volume but consistent niche demand
Clean air zone compliance without AdBlue or DPF complications
Coolant loss from engine block - TSB issued for early examples
Direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves
items.cta: Compare Ford Transit Petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Transit Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: The 2.0L EcoBlue MHEV launched in 2019, adding a belt-integrated starter generator to the standard diesel for energy recovery and enhanced stop-start in urban driving.
items.descriptor: The 2.0L EcoBlue MHEV launched in 2019, adding a belt-integrated starter generator to the standard diesel for energy recovery and enhanced stop-start in urban driving.
families: 48-volt belt-integrated starter generator replaces standard alternator
Recovers braking energy to assist acceleration and power stop-start
Reduces fuel consumption in stop-start urban operation by approx 5-8%
importantNotes: No plug-in hybrid or full electric Transit variant exists for UK market (Transit Electric launched 2022 as BEV - see separate listing)
MHEV engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection and BSG calibration
48V battery replacement adds £400-£800 to standard diesel engine costs
Availability more limited than standard diesel - confirm MHEV compatibility before ordering

engineYears.h2: Ford Transit Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 1965 Mk1 to today's electric E-Transit, this timeline covers every major engine change across the Ford Transit's 60-year production history - helping owners identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Transit that started it all - launched in 1965 with American-inspired styling and a wide track that gave it a huge load capacity advantage over competitors.
keyChanges: First-generation Transit launched (August 1965)
V4 petrol engines from Ford's Essex and Cologne plants
Diesel added in 1972 with long-nose "pig snout" front end
Perkins diesel (1972) replaced by Ford's own York unit
knownFor: 95% of bank raids used Transits - "most wanted van in Britain"
Diesel version needed longer nose - nicknamed "pig snout"
V4 engine design kept front overhang short for better manoeuvrability
First Transit with alternator when rivals still used dynamos
years.cta: Get quotes for 1965-1977 Ford Transit replacement engines
years.ticker: Ford Transit Mk1 engine • Ford Transit York diesel • Ford Transit V4 petrol engine replacement
enquiries: Ford Transit V4 petrol engine replacement

years.description: First major facelift since 1965 - longer nose finally allowed inline engines, replacing the V4s with the familiar Pinto unit from the Cortina.
keyChanges: Major restyling with longer front section (March 1978)
Essex V4 discontinued - replaced by 2.0L Pinto OHC engine
1.6L Kent engine on SWB and crewbus variants
York diesel gained glowplugs (ether start system dropped)
V6 petrol still available as special order
mainEngines: Ford Pinto OHC - 2.0L petrol (76bhp standard / 58bhp derated)
Ford York diesel - 2.4L (glowplug start, improved reliability)
popularModels: Ford Transit SWB / LWB Panel Van
knownFor: First Transit with 2.0L Pinto engine - 10% fuel saving on derated version
York diesel improvements: O-ring oil seal, stronger timing belt, larger water pump bearing
Cold start down to -20°C with new glowplug system
Second millionth Transit produced during this generation
years.cta: Get quotes for 1978-1986 Ford Transit replacement engines
years.ticker: Ford Transit Mk2 engine • Ford Transit 2.0 Pinto • Ford Transit 2.4 York diesel price

years.description: The York engine was completely redesigned in 1984 at a cost of £100 million - becoming the world's first direct injection diesel in a medium-duty commercial vehicle.
keyChanges: York engine redesigned with direct injection (DI) technology
Fuel economy improved to 36.2 mpg (from 27.7 mpg with IDI)
Power increased to 98.6 hp with turbo and EPIC electronic management (1991)
1988: EGR added to meet Euro environmental standards
Mk3 Transit launched 1986 with class-best 0.37 drag coefficient
knownFor: First diesel electronic engine management system (EPIC - Lucas) in any medium-duty commercial
Direct injection reduced heat loss into cylinder head - more power to pistons
24% fuel consumption reduction from IDI to DI
Turbo version with EPIC met increasingly tough Euro emissions standards
years.cta: Get quotes for 1984-2000 Ford Transit engine replacements
years.ticker: Ford Transit 2.5 DI engine • Ford Transit York turbo diesel • Ford Transit Mk3 engine replacement cost
enquiries: Ford Transit Mk3 engine replacement cost

years.description: New platform arrived in 2000 with the Duratorq TDCi - Ford's first common-rail diesel for the Transit, transforming refinement and power delivery.
keyChanges: New platform - first Transit offering both front and rear-wheel drive on same architecture
Duratorq TDCi common-rail diesel introduced
Named "International Van of the Year" 2001
Mk5 facelift in 2006 brought sportier styling and AWD option
knownFor: 2.4L Duratorq D6HA - mechanically simpler than later common-rail units
Oil pump failure risk - documented failure point
Injector pump seal leakage - diesel in engine oil
Robust engine for older van owners who prefer simplicity
years.cta: Compare prices for 2000-2006 Ford Transit Duratorq engines
years.ticker: Ford Transit 2.4 D6HA engine • Ford Transit Mk5 engine replacement • Ford Transit 2006 engine price

years.description: The Mk6 V348 Transit - the highest-volume Transit in UK history. The 2.2L Duratorq (P5AT) dominates replacement demand, with over 60% of UK-registered Mk6 Transits using this engine.
keyChanges: V348 chassis code - Mk6 generation
2.2L Duratorq P5AT became primary engine (85-140hp)
P5AT-LE (low emission) and P5AT-LF (Euro 6) variants
2.4L D6HA continued for legacy applications
Ford AWD option introduced
mainEngines: Duratorq P5AT - 2.2L diesel (85-140hp) - primary UK engine
knownFor: Most searched Transit engine for replacement (2.2L P5AT)
Oil pump drive hex key wear - sudden failure without warning
Injector module failure - common no-start issue
EGR valve clogging on high-mileage examples
Fleet workhorse - still widely used by UK trades
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2014 Ford Transit P5AT engine replacement
years.ticker: Ford Transit 2.2 P5AT engine • Transit Mk6 oil pump failure • Ford Transit 2.2 TDCi rebuilt price • P5AT engine for sale UK

years.description: The V363 platform launched globally across six continents. Ford retained the 2.2L Duratorq but added the heavy-duty 3.2L XLT and introduced EcoBlue towards the end of the generation.
keyChanges: V363 platform - global Transit launched (2014)
2.2L Duratorq continued (CY14 code - updated calibration)
3.2L Duratorq XLT added for heavy-duty 350/460 payloads
2.0L EcoBlue YFAT introduced (2016) - AdBlue injection
First Transit with 6-speed auto transmission option
popularModels: Ford Transit Chassis Cab (FED, FFD codes)
knownFor: 3.2L XLT - largest diesel ever fitted to Transit, max towing capacity
2.0L EcoBlue introduced AdBlue - cleaner but more complex
Euro 6 emissions compliance from 2015 onward
Oil pump drive failure still affected some 2.2L units
years.cta: Compare prices for 2014-2019 Ford Transit engines
years.ticker: Ford Transit 3.2 XLT engine price • Ford Transit 2.0 EcoBlue YFAT • Transit Mk7 2.2 replacement cost

years.description: The 2.0L EcoBlue became the sole diesel offering from 2019, with mHEV adding 48V mild hybrid technology. The E-Transit (2022) marked Ford's first pure electric large van.
keyChanges: 2.2L Duratorq discontinued - 2.0L EcoBlue only diesel
2.0L EcoBlue mHEV (mild hybrid) introduced (2019)
Higher power outputs: 182hp BCFB (from 2019)
E-Transit all-electric launched (2022)
Euro 6.2 emissions compliance
Transit Custom Mk2 on new platform (2023)
knownFor: mHEV uses 48V belt-integrated starter generator
E-Transit - first pure electric Transit (135kW or 198kW)
2.0L EcoBoost petrol - niche option for clean air zones
BCFB 182hp - most powerful standard diesel Transit
Injector clogging and EGR cooler failure documented on early EcoBlue units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-Present Ford Transit engine replacements
years.ticker: Ford Transit 2.0 EcoBlue BCFB • Ford Transit mHEV engine • E-Transit motor repair • Ford Transit 2.0 EcoBoost price

faq.h2: Ford Transit Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Ford Transit engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Ford Transit engine replacement typically costs £1,600-£3,400 for supply only, or £2,200-£4,200 including professional fitting. Main dealer prices run £5,000-£6,000.
keyPoints: Used Transit engines: £800-£1,500 supply only
Reconditioned engines (2.2L Duratorq): £1,600-£2,400 supply only
Rebuilt 2.0L EcoBlue units: £1,800-£2,800 supply only
Full supply & fit package: £2,500-£3,500 typically
Main dealer replacement: £5,000-£6,000 - avoid where possible
items.cta: Compare Ford Transit engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Which Ford Transit engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 2.0L EcoBlue diesel is the most reliable modern Transit engine, with documented 250,000+ mile lifespans and low maintenance costs. The older 2.4L Duratorq (D6HA) is mechanically simpler and rebuild-friendly.
keyPoints: 2.0L EcoBlue diesel: 4.7/5 user satisfaction, 250,000+ mile lifespan
3.5L EcoBoost (heavy duty): 4.5/5 rating, 200,000+ miles
2.4L Duratorq D6HA: simpler mechanical design, easier to rebuild
Avoid early 2.2L P5AT without documented oil pump replacement
items.cta: Get quotes for a reliable rebuilt Ford Transit engine

items.question: What are the common Ford Transit 2.2 Duratorq engine problems?
items.answer: The 2.2L Duratorq (P5AT) has three well-documented failure points: oil pump drive failure, injector module failure, and injector seal leakage leading to diesel-in-oil contamination.
keyPoints: Oil pump drive hex key wears - sudden failure without warning
Injector module failure causes no-start or rough running
Injector seal leaks allow diesel into engine oil - bearing damage follows
Fuel injector recall on some 2014-2015 models (defective internal valve)
EGR valve clogging on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Ford Transit 2.2 P5AT replacement engine prices

items.question: How long does a Ford Transit engine last?
items.answer: A well-maintained Ford Transit diesel engine typically lasts 150,000-250,000 miles. The 2.0L EcoBlue regularly achieves 250,000+ miles, while the 2.2L Duratorq averages 150,000-200,000 miles before major issues.
keyPoints: 2.0L EcoBlue diesel: 250,000+ miles with proper maintenance
2.2L Duratorq P5AT: 150,000-200,000 miles before oil pump or injector issues
3.5L EcoBoost V6 (heavy duty): 200,000+ miles
Regular oil changes critical - extended service intervals risky for high-mileage vans
Preventative oil pump replacement at 80k-100k miles extends life significantly
items.cta: Find a long-life rebuilt Ford Transit engine with warranty

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Ford Transit?
items.answer: Yes, if the van is otherwise solid. A £2,000-£3,000 rebuilt engine can deliver another 100,000+ miles for far less than a replacement van. Commercial operators typically see ROI within 6-12 months.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine costs £1,800-£3,400 versus £15,000-£30,000 for a comparable used Transit
Documented engine swap maintains or boosts resale value
12-month warranty provides commercial operator peace of mind
Most cost-effective when van has no rust or structural damage
Fleet operators save £2,500+ versus dealer replacement
items.cta: Compare Ford Transit engine replacement quotes - supply & fit available

items.question: What causes Ford Transit 2.0 EcoBlue injector failure?
items.answer: Injector failure on 2.0L EcoBlue engines stems from clogged injector nozzles (carbon buildup), seal leakage allowing combustion gases into the fuel return, or electrical module failure within the piezo injector itself.
keyPoints: Carbon clogging from short journeys and poor fuel quality
Injector seal failure - diesel leaks into engine oil, diluting lubrication
Piezoelectric module failure causes intermittent no-start
Original Ford injectors recommended - refurbished units often fail quickly
Symptoms: rough idle, poor acceleration, diesel smell in oil
items.cta: Get Ford Transit 2.0 EcoBlue injector replacement quotes

items.question: Should I buy a used or reconditioned Ford Transit engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines offer better long-term value despite higher upfront cost (£1,600-£2,400 versus £800-£1,500 for used). Used engines carry hidden wear risks and shorter warranties (30-90 days versus 12 months).
keyPoints: Used engine: £800-£1,500, 30-90 day warranty, 30,000-80,000 mile remaining life
Reconditioned engine: £1,600-£2,400, 12-month warranty, upgraded components, 100,000+ mile life
Used engines from written-off vans - service history often unknown
Remanufactured (highest grade): £1,800-£2,500, factory specifications
For business-critical vans, reconditioned is the safer investment
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs used Ford Transit engine prices

items.question: What is the 3.2L Duratorq fuel injection pump recall?
items.answer: 2014-2016 Ford Transit 3.2L Duratorq engines had a fuel injection pump defect causing metallic contamination of the entire fuel system - leading to stalling, no-start, or complete engine failure.
keyPoints: Affects 2014-2016 Transit vans with 3.2L diesel engines
Faulty pump sends metal debris through injectors, rails, and tank
Dealer recall replaced pump, injectors, filter, and flushed system free of charge
If contamination reached crankcase, full engine replacement required
Check recall status before buying used 3.2L Transit
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Transit 3.2 XLT engine replacement

items.question: Can I get a Ford Transit engine fitted at my location (mobile fitting)?
items.answer: Yes, mobile engine fitting is available for Ford Transit vans across most UK major cities and motorway corridors. Specialist fitters come to your workplace or home, typically completing the swap within 1-2 days.
keyPoints: Mobile fitting available within M25, Manchester, Birmingham, Leeds, Bristol corridors
Typical turnaround: 24-48 hours for standard panel van
Collection and delivery options also available for fleet operators
Mobile fitters charge £400-£800 labour versus workshop rates
Most suppliers offer 'supply and fit' as a complete package
items.cta: Find Ford Transit mobile engine fitting specialists near you

items.question: Does a rebuilt Ford Transit engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every reconditioned or rebuilt Ford Transit engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Some specialists offer 24-month or 36,000-mile extended coverage.
keyPoints: Standard warranty: 12 months, unlimited mileage, UK-wide coverage
Premium recon engines: 24 months or 36,000 miles from select suppliers
Used engines: typically 30-90 days only - read terms carefully
Warranty covers manufacturing defects and component failure
Labour costs for re-fixing covered warranty claims varies by supplier
items.cta: Get a warrantied Ford Transit rebuilt engine quote today
